title: Breathing and the brain: Pranayama, an ancient self-directed approach to neuromodulation.

assets.abstract: This paper explores pranayama, the ancient yogic practice of breath control, as a promising natural method of neuromodulation. Pranayama aims to regulate the flow of prana (life force) to enhance mental clarity, emotional balance, and physiological resilience. We synthesize evidence from clinical, neuroimaging, and psychophysiological studies, illustrating how various pranayama techniques - including slow breathing, rapid breathing, and unilateral nostril breathing (UNB) -influence autonomic fun

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Breath and soul — pranayama, breath-brain coupling, respiratory-brain interaction, cardiorespiratory entrainment, vagal pathways of consciousness.
